name: vp-plugins
description: Knowledge graph management and sprint workflow automation for Claude Code

plugins.name: vp-knowledge
plugins.description: Multi-ecosystem package research (npm, Rust, Go, PHP, Python, Ruby) with Socket supply-chain scoring, developer tool research (Homebrew with MCP analytics, GitHub Actions, Docker, VSCode, gh CLI extensions, Claude Code plugins, skills.sh bundles), multi-ecosystem version-drift detection (brew, npm, cask, crate, vscode, plugin), person research, knowledge gap analysis (incl. host-installed plugin/skill coverage), scope-partitioned graph audit/fix skills, project context priming, Readwise integration, session bookmarking, tag vocabulary sync, a Claude Code feature-adoption learning-nudge system, graph maintenance agents, and quality hooks for Basic Memory

plugins.name: vp-beads
plugins.description: Sprint workflow automation: backlog grooming, retrospectives, upstream vendor tracking, cross-project synergy tracking, swarm wave orchestration, vendor sync, and bilateral sibling reconciliation for projects using beads and Basic Memory

plugins.name: vp-git
plugins.description: Git workflow safety skills for stacked-PR chains: rebase-validate (five-layer pipeline — range-diff, sem, weave, jscpd, tests), stack-cascade (safety-tagged rebase --update-refs with atomic force-with-lease push), and tag-audit (SHA-grouped triage of accumulated safety tags with KEEP/DROP confirmation gate). Installable as a Claude Code plugin or via the cross-agent Agent Skills standard (skills.sh)

plugins.name: vp-astgrep
plugins.description: Live ast-grep structural-lint diagnostics via the ast-grep language server: your .ast-grep/rules fire as you edit rather than only on an `ast-grep scan` run, with rule hot-reload and LSP quick-fixes. Claims JavaScript (.js/.mjs/.cjs/.jsx) and bash (.sh/.bash/.zsh) only — when two LSP servers claim one extension the first registered wins and the others never start, so it claims strictly what it lints and can never silently disable pyright/rust-analyzer/gopls (enforced by a check on every build). Requires an sgconfig.yml in the project root and the ast-grep binary on PATH; it is a feedback layer, so keep `ast-grep scan` as the CI gate
